destinations.evidence: Payload building skips fulfilled and delivery settlement candidates when preparation reports missing or inconsistent consensus data.
Short async commitment construction skips when parent context or parent-state validation is unavailable instead of synthesizing placeholder context.

equivalence_argument: The port uses Result-returning preparation/validation and `continue` skip paths for missing commitment, registry, parent-context, or fee context rather than defaulting to zero/fallback values.
notes: Mapped in the port payload builder.
rationale: The primary diff turns update_commitment_tx_with_simulation_context into a Result and skips on lookup failures, checks fulfilled async metadata has commitment_tx and execution_result, changes fallback address/base_fee paths into ok_or_else errors, and uses expect only after prior invariant checks. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Payload building should skip async settlement or delivery settlement transactions when required consensus data is missing, including commitment_tx-derived executor/TEE data, previous block hash/header data for commitment context, or base_fee; it should not synthesize Address::ZERO, zero block placeholders, or a default 20 gwei base fee.
suggested_falsification_tests: Exercise payload building with missing commitment_tx, missing base_fee, and a client that cannot fetch the previous block hash/header. Assert the affected async transaction is skipped and no settlement/commitment transaction with Address::ZERO, zero previous block placeholders, or 20 gwei fallback gas price is included.
summary: Payload builders skip candidates with missing consensus data.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Remove commitment_tx or force unavailable parent context during payload building and assert no settlement or commitment transaction is synthesized.
title: Settlement builders skip instead of using fallback consensus data
